revision.comment: you must let the CFR process play out, and may not preemptively move pages into the redlink in advance of the category actually being moved
revision.text:
{{Short description|Burial mound in Tokyo}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kamenokoyama Kofun | native_name =亀甲山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tamagawadai park kameyama-kofun 0070.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamenokoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Tokyo#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamenokoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|35|31|N|139|40|02|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōta, Tokyo]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th to early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kamenokoyama Kofun'''|亀甲山古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the [[Den-en-chōfu]] neighborhood of [[Ōta, Tokyo]] in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It is one of 54 ''[[kofun]]'' in the Ebaradai ''kofun'' cluster along the banks of the [[Tama River]], and received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1928.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/695|title=亀甲山古墳|trans-title= Kamenokoyama kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamenokoyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the left bank of the lower reaches of the Tama River in the southwestern part of the [[Musashino Terrace]] in southern Tokyo within the grounds of Tamagawadai Park. Although the southern end of the posterior circular mound has been flattened due to the construction of a [[Sewage treatment|water purification plant]], the tumulus remains well preserved. However, no [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is orientated to the northwest, and has a total length of 107.25 meters, which is the largest in the Tama River basin. The mound was built in two tiers. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' nor ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. It is estimated that the tumulus was made in the middle of the Kofun period from the end of the 4th century to the early 5th century AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 107.25 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 10 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 49.5 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Kamenokoyama Kofun is about a 5-minute walk from [[Tamagawa Station (Tokyo)|Tamagawa Station]] on the [[Tōkyū Tōyoko Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tōkyō)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline}} * [https://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/shisetsu/rekishi/minemachi_denenchoufu/kamenokoyama_kofun.html Ōta Ward home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://bunkazai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/jp/search_detail.html?page=1&id=89 Tokyo Cultural Properties Database] {{in lang|ja}}
[[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Tokyo]] [[Category:Ōta, Tokyo]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Musashi Province]]

It is located on the left bank of the lower reaches of the Tama River in the southwestern part of the [[Musashino Terrace]] in southern Tokyo within the grounds of Tamagawadai Park. Although the southern end of the posterior circular mound has been flattened due to the construction of a [[Sewage treatment|water purification plant]], the tumulus remains well preserved. However, no [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is orientated to the northwest, and has a total length of 107.25 meters, which is the largest in the Tama River basin. The mound was built in two tiers. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' nor ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. It is estimated that the tumulus was made in the middle of the Kofun period from the end of the 4th century to the early 5th century AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 107.25 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 10 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 49.5 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Kamenokoyama Kofun is about a 5-minute walk from [[Tamagawa Station (Tokyo)|Tamagawa Station]] on the [[Tōkyū Tōyoko Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Burial mound in Tokyo}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kamenokoyama Kofun | native_name =亀甲山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tamagawadai park kameyama-kofun 0070.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamenokoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Tokyo#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamenokoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|35|31|N|139|40|02|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōta, Tokyo]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th to early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kamenokoyama Kofun'''|亀甲山古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the [[Den-en-chōfu]] neighborhood of [[Ōta, Tokyo]] in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It is one of 54 ''[[kofun]]'' in the Ebaradai ''kofun'' cluster along the banks of the [[Tama River]], and received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1928.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/695|title=亀甲山古墳|trans-title= Kamenokoyama kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamenokoyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the left bank of the lower reaches of the Tama River in the southwestern part of the [[Musashino Terrace]] in southern Tokyo within the grounds of Tamagawadai Park. Although the southern end of the posterior circular mound has been flattened due to the construction of a [[Sewage treatment|water purification plant]], the tumulus remains well preserved. However, no [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is orientated to the northwest, and has a total length of 107.25 meters, which is the largest in the Tama River basin. The mound was built in two tiers. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' nor ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. It is estimated that the tumulus was made in the middle of the Kofun period from the end of the 4th century to the early 5th century AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 107.25 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 10 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 49.5 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Kamenokoyama Kofun is about a 5-minute walk from [[Tamagawa Station (Tokyo)|Tamagawa Station]] on the [[Tōkyū Tōyoko Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Burial mound in Tokyo}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kamenokoyama Kofun | native_name =亀甲山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tamagawadai park kameyama-kofun 0070.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamenokoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Tokyo#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamenokoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|35|31|N|139|40|02|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōta, Tokyo]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th to early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kamenokoyama Kofun'''|亀甲山古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the [[Den-en-chōfu]] neighborhood of [[Ōta, Tokyo]] in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It is one of 54 ''[[kofun]]'' in the Ebaradai ''kofun'' cluster along the banks of the [[Tama River]], and received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1928.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/695|title=亀甲山古墳|trans-title= Kamenokoyama kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamenokoyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the left bank of the lower reaches of the Tama River in the southwestern part of the [[Musashino Terrace]] in southern Tokyo within the grounds of Tamagawadai Park. Although the southern end of the posterior circular mound has been flattened due to the construction of a [[Sewage treatment|water purification plant]], the tumulus remains well preserved. However, no [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is orientated to the northwest, and has a total length of 107.25 meters, which is the largest in the Tama River basin. The mound was built in two tiers. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' nor ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. It is estimated that the tumulus was made in the middle of the Kofun period from the end of the 4th century to the early 5th century AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 107.25 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 10 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 49.5 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Kamenokoyama Kofun is about a 5-minute walk from [[Tamagawa Station (Tokyo)|Tamagawa Station]] on the [[Tōkyū Tōyoko Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tōkyō)]]

{{Short description|Burial mound in Tokyo}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kamenokoyama Kofun | native_name =亀甲山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tamagawadai park kameyama-kofun 0070.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamenokoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Tokyo#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamenokoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|35|31|N|139|40|02|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōta, Tokyo]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th to early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kamenokoyama Kofun'''|亀甲山古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the [[Den-en-chōfu]] neighborhood of [[Ōta, Tokyo]] in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It is one of 54 ''[[kofun]]'' in the Ebaradai ''kofun'' cluster along the banks of the [[Tama River]], and received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1928.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/695|title=亀甲山古墳|trans-title= Kamenokoyama kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamenokoyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the left bank of the lower reaches of the Tama River in the southwestern part of the [[Musashino Terrace]] in southern Tokyo within the grounds of Tamagawadai Park. Although the southern end of the posterior circular mound has been flattened due to the construction of a [[Sewage treatment|water purification plant]], the tumulus remains well preserved. However, no [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is orientated to the northwest, and has a total length of 107.25 meters, which is the largest in the Tama River basin. The mound was built in two tiers. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' nor ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. It is estimated that the tumulus was made in the middle of the Kofun period from the end of the 4th century to the early 5th century AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 107.25 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 10 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 49.5 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Kamenokoyama Kofun is about a 5-minute walk from [[Tamagawa Station (Tokyo)|Tamagawa Station]] on the [[Tōkyū Tōyoko Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Tokyo]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ōta, Tokyo]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Musashi Province]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:kk]]

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kamenokoyama Kofun]][[en:Kamenokoyama Kofun]] [[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[sn:Kamenokoyama Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamenokoyama Kofun}}

{{Short description|Burial mound in Tokyo}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kamenokoyama Kofun | native_name =亀甲山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tamagawadai park kameyama-kofun 0070.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamenokoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Tokyo#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamenokoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|35|31|N|139|40|02|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōta, Tokyo]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th to early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kamenokoyama Kofun'''|亀甲山古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the [[Den-en-chōfu]] neighborhood of [[Ōta, Tokyo]] in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It is one of 54 ''[[kofun]]'' in the Ebaradai ''kofun'' cluster along the banks of the [[Tama River]], and received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1928.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/695|title=亀甲山古墳|trans-title= Kamenokoyama kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamenokoyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the left bank of the lower reaches of the Tama River in the southwestern part of the [[Musashino Terrace]] in southern Tokyo within the grounds of Tamagawadai Park. Although the southern end of the posterior circular mound has been flattened due to the construction of a [[Sewage treatment|water purification plant]], the tumulus remains well preserved. However, no [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is orientated to the northwest, and has a total length of 107.25 meters, which is the largest in the Tama River basin. The mound was built in two tiers. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' nor ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. It is estimated that the tumulus was made in the miqqle of the Kofun period from the end of the 4th century to the early 5th century AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 107.25 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 10 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 49.5 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Kamenokoyama Kofun is about a 5-minute walk from [[Tamagawa Station (Tokyo)|Tamagawa Station]] on the [[Tōkyū Tōyoko Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun in Tokyo}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kamenokoyama Kofun | native_name =亀甲山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tamagawadai park kameyama-kofun 0070.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamenokoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Tokyo#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamenokoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|35|31|N|139|40|02|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōta, Tokyo]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th to early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kamenokoyama Kofun'''|亀甲山古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the [[Den-en-chōfu]] neighborhood of [[Ōta, Tokyo]] in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It is one of 54 ''[[kofun]]'' in the Ebaradai ''kofun'' cluster along the banks of the [[Tama River]], and received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1928.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/695|title=亀甲山古墳|trans-title= Kamenokoyama kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamenokoyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the left bank of the lower reaches of the Tama River in the southwestern part of the [[Musashino Terrace]] in southern Tokyo within the grounds of Tamagawadai Park. Although the southern end of the posterior circular mound has been flattened due to the construction of a [[Sewage treatment|water purification plant]], the tumulus remains well preserved. However, no [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is orientated to the northwest, and has a total length of 107.25 meters, which is the largest in the Tama River basin. The mound was built in two tiers. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' nor ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. It is estimated that the tumulus was made in the miqqle of the Kofun period from the end of the 4th century to the early 5th century AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 107.25 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 10 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 49.5 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Kamenokoyama Kofun is about a 5-minute walk from [[Tamagawa Station (Tokyo)|Tamagawa Station]] on the [[Tōkyū Tōyoko Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

It is estimated that the tumulus was made in the middle[[Category:middle]] of the Kofun period from the end of the 4th century to the early 5th century AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

It is located on the left bank of the lower reaches of the Tama River in the southwestern part of the [[Musashino Terrace]] in southern Tokyo within the grounds of Tamagawadai Park. Although the southern end of the posterior circular mound has been flattened due to the construction of a [[Sewage treatment|water purification plant]], the tumulus remains well preserved. However, no [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is orientated to the northwest, and has a total length of 107.25 meters, which is the largest in the Tama River basin. The mound was built in two tiers. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' nor ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. It is estimated that the tumulus was made in the middle[[Category:middle]] of the Kofun period from the end of the 4th century to the early 5th century AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 107.25 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 10 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 49.5 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Kamenokoyama Kofun is about a 5-minute walk from [[Tamagawa Station (Tokyo)|Tamagawa Station]] on the [[Tōkyū Tōyoko Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun in Tokyo}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kamenokoyama Kofun | native_name =亀甲山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tamagawadai park kameyama-kofun 0070.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamenokoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Tokyo#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamenokoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|35|31|N|139|40|02|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōta, Tokyo]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th to early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kamenokoyama Kofun'''|亀甲山古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the [[Den-en-chōfu]] neighborhood of [[Ōta, Tokyo]] in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It is one of 54 ''[[kofun]]'' in the Ebaradai ''kofun'' cluster along the banks of the [[Tama River]], and received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1928.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/695|title=亀甲山古墳|trans-title= Kamenokoyama kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamenokoyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the left bank of the lower reaches of the Tama River in the southwestern part of the [[Musashino Terrace]] in southern Tokyo within the grounds of Tamagawadai Park. Although the southern end of the posterior circular mound has been flattened due to the construction of a [[Sewage treatment|water purification plant]], the tumulus remains well preserved. However, no [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is orientated to the northwest, and has a total length of 107.25 meters, which is the largest in the Tama River basin. The mound was built in two tiers. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' nor ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. It is estimated that the tumulus was made in the middle[[Category:middle]] of the Kofun period from the end of the 4th century to the early 5th century AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 107.25 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 10 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 49.5 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Kamenokoyama Kofun is about a 5-minute walk from [[Tamagawa Station (Tokyo)|Tamagawa Station]] on the [[Tōkyū Tōyoko Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tōkyō)]]

{{Short description|Kofun in Tokyo}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kamenokoyama Kofun | native_name =亀甲山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tamagawadai park kameyama-kofun 0070.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamenokoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Tokyo#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamenokoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|35|31|N|139|40|02|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōta, Tokyo]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th to early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kamenokoyama Kofun'''|亀甲山古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the [[Den-en-chōfu]] neighborhood of [[Ōta, Tokyo]] in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It is one of 54 ''[[kofun]]'' in the Ebaradai ''kofun'' cluster along the banks of the [[Tama River]], and received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1928.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/695|title=亀甲山古墳|trans-title= Kamenokoyama kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamenokoyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the left bank of the lower reaches of the Tama River in the southwestern part of the [[Musashino Terrace]] in southern Tokyo within the grounds of Tamagawadai Park. Although the southern end of the posterior circular mound has been flattened due to the construction of a [[Sewage treatment|water purification plant]], the tumulus remains well preserved. However, no [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is orientated to the northwest, and has a total length of 107.25 meters, which is the largest in the Tama River basin. The mound was built in two tiers. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' nor ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. It is estimated that the tumulus was made in the middleof the Kofun period from the end of the 4th century to the early 5th century AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 107.25 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 10 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 49.5 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Kamenokoyama Kofun is about a 5-minute walk from [[Tamagawa Station (Tokyo)|Tamagawa Station]] on the [[Tōkyū Tōyoko Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tōkyō)]]

It is located on the left bank of the lower reaches of the Tama River in the southwestern part of the [[Musashino Terrace]] in southern Tokyo within the grounds of Tamagawadai Park. Although the southern end of the posterior circular mound has been flattened due to the construction of a [[Sewage treatment|water purification plant]], the tumulus remains well preserved. However, no [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is orientated to the northwest, and has a total length of 107.25 meters, which is the largest in the Tama River basin. The mound was built in two tiers. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' nor ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. It is estimated that the tumulus was made in the middleof the Kofun period from the end of the 4th century to the early 5th century AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 107.25 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 10 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 49.5 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Kamenokoyama Kofun is about a 5-minute walk from [[Tamagawa Station (Tokyo)|Tamagawa Station]] on the [[Tōkyū Tōyoko Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

However, no [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is orientated to the northwest, and has a total length of 107.25 meters, which is the largest in the Tama River basin. The mound was built in two tiers. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' nor ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. It is estimated that the tumulus was made in the middleof the Kofun period from the end of the 4th century to the early 5th century AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 107.25 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 10 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 49.5 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Kamenokoyama Kofun is about a 5-minute walk from [[Tamagawa Station (Tokyo)|Tamagawa Station]] on the [[Tōkyū Tōyoko Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' nor ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. It is estimated that the tumulus was made in the middleof the Kofun period from the end of the 4th century to the early 5th century AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The Kamenokoyama Kofun is about a 5-minute walk from [[Tamagawa Station (Tokyo)|Tamagawa Station]] on the [[Tōkyū Tōyoko Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

However, no [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is orientated to the northwest, and has a total length of 107.25 meters, which is the largest in the Tama River basin. The mound was built in two tiers. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' nor ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. It is estimated that the tumulus was made in the middleof the Kofun period from the end of the 4th century to the early 5th century AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 107.25 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 10 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 49.5 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Kamenokoyama Kofun is about a 5-minute walk from [[Tamagawa Station (Tokyo)|Tamagawa Station]] on the [[Tōkyū Tōyoko Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun in Tokyo}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kamenokoyama Kofun | native_name =亀甲山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tamagawadai park kameyama-kofun 0070.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamenokoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Tokyo#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamenokoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|35|31|N|139|40|02|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Ōta, Tokyo|Ōta, Tokyo]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th to early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kamenokoyama Kofun'''|亀甲山古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the [[:en:Den-en-chōfu|Den-en-chōfu]] neighborhood of [[:en:Ōta, Tokyo|Ōta, Tokyo]] in the [[Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is one of 54 ''[[kofun]]'' in the Ebaradai ''kofun'' cluster along the banks of the [[Tama River]], and received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1928.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/695|title=亀甲山古墳|trans-title= Kamenokoyama kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamenokoyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the left bank of the lower reaches of the Tama River in the southwestern part of the [[Musashino Terrace]] in southern Tokyo within the grounds of Tamagawadai Park. Although the southern end of the posterior circular mound has been flattened due to the construction of a [[Sewage treatment|water purification plant]], the tumulus remains well preserved. However, no [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is orientated to the northwest, and has a total length of 107.25 meters, which is the largest in the Tama River basin. The mound was built in two tiers. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' nor ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. It is estimated that the tumulus was made in the middleof the Kofun period from the end of the 4th century to the early 5th century AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 107.25 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 10 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 49.5 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Kamenokoyama Kofun is about a 5-minute walk from [[Tamagawa Station (Tokyo)|Tamagawa Station]] on the [[Tōkyū Tōyoko Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun in Tokyo}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kamenokoyama Kofun | native_name =亀甲山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tamagawadai park kameyama-kofun 0070.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamenokoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Tokyo#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamenokoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|35|31|N|139|40|02|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Ōta, Tokyo|Ōta, Tokyo]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th to early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kamenokoyama Kofun'''|亀甲山古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the [[:en:Den-en-chōfu|Den-en-chōfu]] neighborhood of [[:en:Ōta, Tokyo|Ōta, Tokyo]] in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is one of 54 ''[[kofun]]'' in the Ebaradai ''kofun'' cluster along the banks of the [[:en:Tama River|Tama River]], and received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1928.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/695|title=亀甲山古墳|trans-title= Kamenokoyama kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamenokoyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the left bank of the lower reaches of the Tama River in the southwestern part of the [[Musashino Terrace]] in southern Tokyo within the grounds of Tamagawadai Park. Although the southern end of the posterior circular mound has been flattened due to the construction of a [[Sewage treatment|water purification plant]], the tumulus remains well preserved. However, no [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is orientated to the northwest, and has a total length of 107.25 meters, which is the largest in the Tama River basin. The mound was built in two tiers. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' nor ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. It is estimated that the tumulus was made in the middleof the Kofun period from the end of the 4th century to the early 5th century AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 107.25 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 10 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 49.5 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Kamenokoyama Kofun is about a 5-minute walk from [[:en:Tamagawa Station (Tokyo)|Tamagawa Station]] on the [[Tōkyū Tōyoko Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tōkyō)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tōkyō)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:11]][[Category:-inline}} * [https://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/shisetsu/rekishi/minemachi_denenchoufu/kamenokoyama_kofun.html Ōta Ward home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://bunkazai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/jp/search_detail.html?page=1&id=89 Tokyo Cultural Properties Database] {{in lang|ja}}

revision.comment: Bot: prepend Wikidata AFC comment via pages.txt
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11371013]] Wikidata name: Kamenokoyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun in Tokyo}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kamenokoyama Kofun | native_name =亀甲山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tamagawadai park kameyama-kofun 0070.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamenokoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Tokyo#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamenokoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|35|31|N|139|40|02|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Ōta, Tokyo|Ōta, Tokyo]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th to early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kamenokoyama Kofun'''|亀甲山古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the [[:en:Den-en-chōfu|Den-en-chōfu]] neighborhood of [[:en:Ōta, Tokyo|Ōta, Tokyo]] in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is one of 54 ''[[kofun]]'' in the Ebaradai ''kofun'' cluster along the banks of the [[:en:Tama River|Tama River]], and received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1928.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/695|title=亀甲山古墳|trans-title= Kamenokoyama kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamenokoyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the left bank of the lower reaches of the Tama River in the southwestern part of the [[Musashino Terrace]] in southern Tokyo within the grounds of Tamagawadai Park. Although the southern end of the posterior circular mound has been flattened due to the construction of a [[:en:Sewage treatment|water purification plant]], the tumulus remains well preserved. However, no [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is orientated to the northwest, and has a total length of 107.25 meters, which is the largest in the Tama River basin. The mound was built in two tiers. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' nor ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. It is estimated that the tumulus was made in the middleof the Kofun period from the end of the 4th century to the early 5th century AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 107.25 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 10 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 49.5 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Kamenokoyama Kofun is about a 5-minute walk from [[:en:Tamagawa Station (Tokyo)|Tamagawa Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkyū Tōyoko Line|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tōkyō)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tōkyō)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:11]][[Category:-inline}} * [https://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/shisetsu/rekishi/minemachi_denenchoufu/kamenokoyama_kofun.html Ōta Ward home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://bunkazai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/jp/search_detail.html?page=1&id=89 Tokyo Cultural Properties Database] {{in lang|ja}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11371013]] Wikidata name: Kamenokoyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11371013]] Wikidata name: Kamenokoyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun in Tokyo}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kamenokoyama Kofun | native_name =亀甲山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Kamenokoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamenokoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Tokyo#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamenokoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.589222|N|139.667083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Den'en Chōfu|qid=Q2613265}} | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of ={{ill|Ebaradai kofun-gun|qid=Q11618305}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th to early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kamenokoyama Kofun'''|亀甲山古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the [[:en:Den-en-chōfu|Den-en-chōfu]] neighborhood of [[:en:Ōta, Tokyo|Ōta, Tokyo]] in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is one of 54 ''[[kofun]]'' in the Ebaradai ''kofun'' cluster along the banks of the [[:en:Tama River|Tama River]], and received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1928.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/695|title=亀甲山古墳|trans-title= Kamenokoyama kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamenokoyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the left bank of the lower reaches of the Tama River in the southwestern part of the [[Musashino Terrace]] in southern Tokyo within the grounds of Tamagawadai Park. Although the southern end of the posterior circular mound has been flattened due to the construction of a [[:en:Sewage treatment|water purification plant]], the tumulus remains well preserved. However, no [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is orientated to the northwest, and has a total length of 107.25 meters, which is the largest in the Tama River basin. The mound was built in two tiers. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' nor ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. It is estimated that the tumulus was made in the middleof the Kofun period from the end of the 4th century to the early 5th century AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 107.25 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 10 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 49.5 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Kamenokoyama Kofun is about a 5-minute walk from [[:en:Tamagawa Station (Tokyo)|Tamagawa Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkyū Tōyoko Line|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tōkyō)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tōkyō)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links==
* [https://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/shisetsu/rekishi/minemachi_denenchoufu/kamenokoyama_kofun.html Ōta Ward home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://bunkazai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/jp/search_detail.html?page=1&id=89 Tokyo Cultural Properties Database] {{in lang|ja}}

[[kofun]] | part_of ={{ill|Ebaradai kofun-gun|WD=Q11618305}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th to early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kamenokoyama Kofun'''|亀甲山古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the [[:en:Den-en-chōfu|Den-en-chōfu]] neighborhood of [[:en:Ōta, Tokyo|Ōta, Tokyo]] in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is one of 54 ''[[kofun]]'' in the Ebaradai ''kofun'' cluster along the banks of the [[:en:Tama River|Tama River]], and received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1928.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/695|title=亀甲山古墳|trans-title= Kamenokoyama kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamenokoyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the left bank of the lower reaches of the Tama River in the southwestern part of the [[Musashino Terrace]] in southern Tokyo within the grounds of Tamagawadai Park. Although the southern end of the posterior circular mound has been flattened due to the construction of a [[:en:Sewage treatment|water purification plant]], the tumulus remains well preserved. However, no [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is orientated to the northwest, and has a total length of 107.25 meters, which is the largest in the Tama River basin. The mound was built in two tiers. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' nor ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. It is estimated that the tumulus was made in the middleof the Kofun period from the end of the 4th century to the early 5th century AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 107.25 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 10 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 49.5 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Kamenokoyama Kofun is about a 5-minute walk from [[:en:Tamagawa Station (Tokyo)|Tamagawa Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkyū Tōyoko Line|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tōkyō)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tōkyō)]]

{{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kamenokoyama Kofun'''|亀甲山古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the [[:en:Den-en-chōfu|Den-en-chōfu]] neighborhood of [[:en:Ōta, Tokyo|Ōta, Tokyo]] in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is one of 54 ''[[kofun]]'' in the Ebaradai ''kofun'' cluster along the banks of the [[:en:Tama River|Tama River]], and received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1928.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/695|title=亀甲山古墳|trans-title= Kamenokoyama kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamenokoyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the left bank of the lower reaches of the Tama River in the southwestern part of the [[Musashino Terrace]] in southern Tokyo within the grounds of Tamagawadai Park. Although the southern end of the posterior circular mound has been flattened due to the construction of a [[:en:Sewage treatment|water purification plant]], the tumulus remains well preserved. However, no [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is orientated to the northwest, and has a total length of 107.25 meters, which is the largest in the Tama River basin. The mound was built in two tiers. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' nor ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. It is estimated that the tumulus was made in the middleof the Kofun period from the end of the 4th century to the early 5th century AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 107.25 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 10 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 49.5 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Kamenokoyama Kofun is about a 5-minute walk from [[:en:Tamagawa Station (Tokyo)|Tamagawa Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkyū Tōyoko Line|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tōkyō)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tōkyō)]]

{{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kamenokoyama Kofun'''|亀甲山古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the [[:en:Den-en-chōfu|Den-en-chōfu]] neighborhood of [[:en:Ōta, Tokyo|Ōta, Tokyo]] in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is one of 54 ''[[kofun]]'' in the Ebaradai ''kofun'' cluster along the banks of the [[:en:Tama River|Tama River]], and received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1928.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/695|title=亀甲山古墳|trans-title= Kamenokoyama kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamenokoyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the left bank of the lower reaches of the Tama River in the southwestern part of the [[Musashino Terrace]] in southern Tokyo within the grounds of Tamagawadai Park. Although the southern end of the posterior circular mound has been flattened due to the construction of a [[:en:Sewage treatment|water purification plant]], the tumulus remains well preserved. However, no [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is orientated to the northwest, and has a total length of 107.25 meters, which is the largest in the Tama River basin. The mound was built in two tiers. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' nor ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. It is estimated that the tumulus was made in the middleof the Kofun period from the end of the 4th century to the early 5th century AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 107.25 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 10 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 49.5 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Kamenokoyama Kofun is about a 5-minute walk from [[:en:Tamagawa Station (Tokyo)|Tamagawa Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkyū Tōyoko Line|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamenokoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Tokyo#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamenokoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.589222|N|139.667083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Den'en Chōfu|WD=Q2613265}} | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of ={{ill|Ebaradai kofun-gun|WD=Q11618305}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th to early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kamenokoyama Kofun'''|亀甲山古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the [[:en:Den-en-chōfu|Den-en-chōfu]] neighborhood of [[:en:Ōta, Tokyo|Ōta, Tokyo]] in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is one of 54 ''[[kofun]]'' in the Ebaradai ''kofun'' cluster along the banks of the [[:en:Tama River|Tama River]], and received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1928.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/695|title=亀甲山古墳|trans-title= Kamenokoyama kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamenokoyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the left bank of the lower reaches of the Tama River in the southwestern part of the [[Musashino Terrace]] in southern Tokyo within the grounds of Tamagawadai Park. Although the southern end of the posterior circular mound has been flattened due to the construction of a [[:en:Sewage treatment|water purification plant]], the tumulus remains well preserved. However, no [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is orientated to the northwest, and has a total length of 107.25 meters, which is the largest in the Tama River basin. The mound was built in two tiers. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' nor ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. It is estimated that the tumulus was made in the middleof the Kofun period from the end of the 4th century to the early 5th century AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 107.25 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 10 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 49.5 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Kamenokoyama Kofun is about a 5-minute walk from [[:en:Tamagawa Station (Tokyo)|Tamagawa Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkyū Tōyoko Line|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tōkyō)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tōkyō)]]

{{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kamenokoyama Kofun'''|亀甲山古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the [[:en:Den-en-chōfu|Den-en-chōfu]] neighborhood of [[:en:Ōta, Tokyo|Ōta, Tokyo]] in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is one of 54 ''[[kofun]]'' in the Ebaradai ''kofun'' cluster along the banks of the [[:en:Tama River|Tama River]], and received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1928.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/695|title=亀甲山古墳|trans-title= Kamenokoyama kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamenokoyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the left bank of the lower reaches of the Tama River in the southwestern part of the [[Musashino Terrace]] in southern Tokyo within the grounds of Tamagawadai Park. Although the southern end of the posterior circular mound has been flattened due to the construction of a [[:en:Sewage treatment|water purification plant]], the tumulus remains well preserved. However, no [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is orientated to the northwest, and has a total length of 107.25 meters, which is the largest in the Tama River basin. The mound was built in two tiers. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' nor ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. It is estimated that the tumulus was made in the middleof the Kofun period from the end of the 4th century to the early 5th century AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 107.25 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 10 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 49.5 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Kamenokoyama Kofun is about a 5-minute walk from [[:en:Tamagawa Station (Tokyo)|Tamagawa Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkyū Tōyoko Line|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tōkyō)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tōkyō)]]

It is estimated that the tumulus was made in the middleof the Kofun period from the end of the 4th century to the early 5th century AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 107.25 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 10 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 49.5 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Kamenokoyama Kofun is about a 5-minute walk from {{ill|Tamagawa Station (Tokyo)|en|Tamagawa Station (Tokyo)|WD=Q7680540|lt=Tamagawa Station}} on the [[:en:Tōkyū Tōyoko Line|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It is one of 54 ''[[kofun]]'' in the Ebaradai ''kofun'' cluster along the banks of the {{ill|Tama River|en|Tama River|WD=Q1063006|lt=Tama River}}, and received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1928.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/695|title=亀甲山古墳|trans-title= Kamenokoyama kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamenokoyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the left bank of the lower reaches of the Tama River in the southwestern part of the [[Musashino Terrace]] in southern Tokyo within the grounds of Tamagawadai Park. Although the southern end of the posterior circular mound has been flattened due to the construction of a {{ill|Sewage treatment|en|Sewage treatment|WD=Q221275|lt=water purification plant}}, the tumulus remains well preserved. However, no [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is orientated to the northwest, and has a total length of 107.25 meters, which is the largest in the Tama River basin. The mound was built in two tiers. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' nor ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been found. It is estimated that the tumulus was made in the middleof the Kofun period from the end of the 4th century to the early 5th century AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 107.25 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 10 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 49.5 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Kamenokoyama Kofun is about a 5-minute walk from {{ill|Tamagawa Station (Tokyo)|en|Tamagawa Station (Tokyo)|WD=Q7680540|lt=Tamagawa Station}} on the [[:en:Tōkyū Tōyoko Line|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kamenokoyama Kofun'''|亀甲山古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the {{ill|Den-en-chōfu|en|Den-en-chōfu|WD=Q2613265|lt=Den-en-chōfu}} neighborhood of {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en|Ōta, Tokyo|WD=Q217234|lt=Ōta, Tokyo}} in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It is one of 54 ''[[kofun]]'' in the Ebaradai ''kofun'' cluster along the banks of the {{ill|Tama River|en|Tama River|WD=Q1063006|lt=Tama River}}, and received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1928.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/695|title=亀甲山古墳|trans-title= Kamenokoyama kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamenokoyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the left bank of the lower reaches of the Tama River in the southwestern part of the [[Musashino Terrace]] in southern Tokyo within the grounds of Tamagawadai Park. Although the southern end of the posterior circular mound has been flattened due to the construction of a {{ill|Sewage treatment|en|Sewage treatment|WD=Q221275|lt=water purification plant}}, the tumulus remains well preserved. However, no [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is orientated to the northwest, and has a total length of 107.25 meters, which is the largest in the Tama River basin. The mound was built in two tiers. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' nor ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been found. It is estimated that the tumulus was made in the middleof the Kofun period from the end of the 4th century to the early 5th century AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 107.25 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 10 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 49.5 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Kamenokoyama Kofun is about a 5-minute walk from {{ill|Tamagawa Station (Tokyo)|en|Tamagawa Station (Tokyo)|WD=Q7680540|lt=Tamagawa Station}} on the [[:en:Tōkyū Tōyoko Line|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tōkyō)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tōkyō)]]

{{Short description|Kofun in Tokyo}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kamenokoyama Kofun | native_name =亀甲山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Kamenokoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamenokoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Tokyo#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamenokoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.589222|N|139.667083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Den'en Chōfu|WD=Q2613265|1=Den-en-chōfu}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of ={{ill|Ebaradai kofun-gun|WD=Q11618305|1=Ebaradai kofun-gun}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th to early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kamenokoyama Kofun'''|亀甲山古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the {{ill|Den-en-chōfu|en|Den-en-chōfu|WD=Q2613265|lt=Den-en-chōfu|1=Den-en-chōfu}} neighborhood of {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en|Ōta, Tokyo|WD=Q217234|lt=Ōta, Tokyo|1=Ōta, Tokyo}} in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is one of 54 ''[[kofun]]'' in the Ebaradai ''kofun'' cluster along the banks of the {{ill|Tama River|en|Tama River|WD=Q1063006|lt=Tama River|1=Tama River}}, and received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1928.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/695|title=亀甲山古墳|trans-title= Kamenokoyama kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamenokoyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the left bank of the lower reaches of the Tama River in the southwestern part of the [[Musashino Terrace]] in southern Tokyo within the grounds of Tamagawadai Park. Although the southern end of the posterior circular mound has been flattened due to the construction of a {{ill|Sewage treatment|en|Sewage treatment|WD=Q221275|lt=water purification plant|1=Sewage treatment}}, the tumulus remains well preserved. However, no [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is orientated to the northwest, and has a total length of 107.25 meters, which is the largest in the Tama River basin. The mound was built in two tiers. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' nor ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found. It is estimated that the tumulus was made in the middleof the Kofun period from the end of the 4th century to the early 5th century AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 107.25 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 10 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 49.5 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Kamenokoyama Kofun is about a 5-minute walk from {{ill|Tamagawa Station (Tokyo)|en|Tamagawa Station (Tokyo)|WD=Q7680540|lt=Tamagawa Station|1=Tamagawa Station (Tokyo)}} on the [[:en:Tōkyū Tōyoko Line|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tōkyō)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tōkyō)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links==
